key_evidence: The published 6.3 predates the Nov-2025 democratic transition; under the same logic as the Venezuela special case, the score should reflect the conduct of the elected Paz government.

A functioning elected democracy managing an economic crisis (with a public ombudsman, retained social programs, and emergency-assistance operations) cannot defensibly sit at ~6/100 alongside active-atrocity states.

Genuine negatives remain: a 90-day military state of emergency, fuel-subsidy hardship, and ≥ 17 deaths from disrupted medical access — keeping the assessed score conservatively within the Critical band.
Assessed 15.9 vs published 6.3 (delta +9.6) — CHANGE PROPOSAL (upgrade), no band crossing (Critical -> Critical). Confidence medium given volatility and desk-review limits.

evidence.quote: Bolivians elected Rodrigo Paz of the centre-right Christian Democratic Party (PDC) as their new president, ending almost 20 years of governance by the Movement for Socialism (MAS) party.
evidence.claim: Bolivia underwent a peaceful democratic transition in late 2025, ending 20 years of one-party rule — the governing reality the published 6.3 (set pre-transition) does not reflect.

evidence.quote: Rodrigo Paz sworn-in as Bolivia's new president ending 20 year dynasty of one-party rule
evidence.claim: The elected Paz government was sworn in November 2025; the current score should reflect its conduct, not the prior regime's.

evidence.quote: the Bolivian Ombudsman's Office attributes at least 17 deaths to disrupted medical access
evidence.claim: Genuine within-window harm persists (≥17 deaths from disrupted medical access during a 90-day military state of emergency), keeping the assessed score conservatively within the Critical band.

evidence.quote: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz declared a state of emergency, which gives the military the power to remove blockades that have disrupted food and fuel supplies.
evidence.claim: A functioning elected state is actively managing the crisis (emergency declaration, military road-clearing, retained social benefits) — conduct materially above the deep-critical 6.3 baseline.

rejection_reason: False positive — fails 3e-bis baseline-provenance + directionality screening. The proposal's premise (that the 6.3 is a stale baseline predating Bolivia's democratic transition) is factually wrong: the score was driven down May–June 2026 under the elected Paz government by its OWN documented conduct (Jun 8 codified-impunity law authorizing military force on protesters; 10+ killed, 365+ arrested, terrorism charges against union leaders; subsequent within-Critical deepening to 6.3). Within-window evidence this cycle is negative (90-day military state of emergency; 17 deaths incl. 7 from blocked medical access; Jun 22 relief-flight crash killing 6), so no positive countervailing conduct justifies an upgrade. Bolivia CONFIRMED at 6.3. Any view that 6.3 is mis-calibrated vs the floor cluster is a flag-for-review calibration question, not a one-off reset.
